text:
OWASP published an MCP Top 10. 30+ CVEs, CVSS 9.6 RCE, 43% exec/shell injection.

all 10 are runtime security. none cover build-time schema quality.

tool poisoning starts in the schema, not at runtime. the description field is already an attack surface.

prompt override detection is one of our 13 validation checks.

it looks for: instruction framing, imperative commands, persona switches, information suppression. in the description field of an MCP tool schema.

OWASP missed this. it's already happening in the wild.

we graded 201 MCP servers. the most popular ones are the worst.

Context7: F. GitHub official: F. Blender: F. Notion: F.

these are the servers your agents are probably already using.

https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html
